rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DANIEL M. POMMON JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/05/2024 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No financial account statements verifying cash balances as of 09/20/2023 are on file. 3) The proof of service does not show notice was given to the attorney for the person who requested special notice. 4) The petitioner appears to have paid herself $1,440 as fees on account prior to the conservatee's death, in addition to $10,800 paid during the supplemental account period. The total unpaid balance must be further reduced by $1,440. 5) The ending balance of the supplemental account period stated on the summary of account does not match the ending balance stated on Schedules E & F. 6) A specific amount of net cash distributable to the personal representative of the conservatee's estate, after payment of fees and costs sought to be approved, must be stated in the petition and proposed order. 7) The court-appointed attorney for the conservatee has not petitioned for fees and costs, although her creditor claim was filed in the conservatee's probate estate case more than 2 years ago.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/10/2024 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 02/26/2024, 03/18/2024, and 04/10/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) Notice must be given to the successors of the postdeceased beneficiaries who have not signed a power of attorney authorizing Kemp & Associates to receive notice, as the powers of attorney executed by the postdeceased beneficiaries are no longer effective. 2) An original small estate affidavit (13100 declaration) signed by all the successors of Azuceno Olorvida must be filed with a certified copy of his death certificate. The affidavit must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). LRSF 14.59(C)(2). 3) The statements of the Chase, US Bank, and Bank of America accounts must cover periods through and including 06/30/2023, the ending date of the account. Also, the attorney's declaration filed on 03/08/2024 did not include language required by CCP section 2015.5 (either a place of execution within California or "under the laws of the State of California" must be stated). 4) No prior court approval has been obtained to retain the real estate attorney and make payment from the estate. 5) Are the services performed by California Inheritance Group ordinary services that should have been performed by the administrator? If so, should CIG's 10% fee paid from the estate be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation? 6) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee, assignee, and alternate distributee required to be identified by Probate Code section 11603(c). The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 7) A review of a copy of the closing escrow statement attached to the supplement filed on 03/05/2024 shows the total costs of $76,734.85, but Schedule E attached to the petition shows $77.535.21. 8) There is only one witness to the powers of attorney purportedly signed by Edlen Alayon. Direct notice must be given to him or her if the power of attorney is invalid. 9) There are no signatures of the witnesses to the power of attorney purportedly signed by Victor Beltran. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 10) The power of attorney purportedly signed by Arnold Olorvida with his fingerprint was not dated. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 11) There are no assignments signed by Salome Olorvida, Cristy Amoro, Allan A.S. Olorvida, Daniel Olorvida, Gliza Olorvida, Franz Olorvida, Artemio loysaynon, Agapito Losaynon, and Artemio Losaynon. Note: No reserve for closing expenses has been requested.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEVIN JOHN O'DONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petitioner's attorney previously represented Patricia Hill, the executor whose petition for final distribution was granted by the Court on 10/09/2023. The executor died before making distribution of assets (other than real property) and payment of fees and other expenses authorized and directed by the Court's 10/09/2023 final distribution order. The final distribution order identified the petitioner as personal representative of the estate of Hirokazu Shinohara, the sole distributee of the estate assets on hand. The petitioner's attorney, who presumably prepared the deceased executor's petition for final distribution in August 2023, disclosed in her supplement to this petition that the petitioner has never been appointed as personal representative of Hirokazu Shinohara's estate. The final distribution order requiring distribution to the estate of Hirokazu Shinohara was not timely appealed or set aside under CCP section 473(b). The Court may determine: 1) whether to require appointment of a personal representative of the estate of Hirokazu Shinohara in compliance with the final distribution order; 2) whetehr to grant special administration with limited powers to carry out the tasks set forth in the final distribution order; 3) whehter to require the petitioner to give a full bond ($133,000) absent a bond waiver signed by Hirokazu Shinohara's personal representative in that capacity; and/or 4) The final distribution order should be set aside based on misrepresentation as to the petitioner's status as Hirokazu Shinohara's personal representative so identified in the deceased executor's petition for final distribution. See Probate Code section 7250(c) defining "misrepresentation" to include the omission of a material fact. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BRUCE M. AND CYNTHIA R. LUBARSKY TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petiton For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/05/2024 (no earlier date available). A review of the supplement filed on 04/19/2024 indicates that the following issues must be addressed: 1) The Bank of America account 9943 sought to be confirmed as additional property of the trust was not listed in the petition. The petition must be amended and renoticed. 2) If the Wells Fargo account 8758 was titled in Rico Realty Corp's name, the corporation must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. (including 416.10 or 416.20). If shares of stock of Rico issued to the deceased settlor in his individual name (or his right to receive corporate distributon from Rico as its shareholder) need be confirmed as property of the trust, the petition must be amended and renoticed. 3) No statement of the Bank of America account 1436 was attached to the supplement. If this account is truly an attorney-client trust account, funds in the account belong to the deceased settlor's clients. A practice administrator must be appointed to take control of the deceased settlor's law practice and assets including client trust accounts. See Probate Code sections 9764 and 17200(b)(22); see also Business & Professions Code section 6185. 4) The Bank of America account 0027 was opened in November 2022 and did not exist when the deceased settlor signed the restated trust with a schedule of trust assets in August 2021. 5) The Bank of America safe deposit box location and account number are different from those listed in the petition. It is unclear whether the same contents were transferred, and if not, whether the safe deposit box listed in the petition was opened before or after the August 2021 trust restatement.
